planner.summary: Root cause identified: PDF flow creates TWO bot messages (notificationMsg + userConfirmMsg) at lines 424-428 BEFORE calling LlmChatModelHelper.sendMessage(). The thinking callback appends to the last bot message (userConfirmMsg) which already contains workspace info text, while the actual model response ALSO appends to userConfirmMsg. This creates two visual 'stuck' messages: one showing thinking bubbles with accumulated thinking text, and one showing the workspace confirmation with response tokens — both appearing before the final response properly renders.
planner.problem_statement: When sending a PDF, two 'locked/stuck' messages appear at the beginning before thinking runs, then the final response. The user sees: (1) a message with the workspace confirmation text, (2) a message showing thinking bubbles with accumulated thinking text — both appearing stuck before the final response flows into the proper message.

evidence.detail: Lines 379-381: loadingMsg = ChatMessage(text='📄 Processando PDF...', isUser=false) is added. Lines 406: loading message removed. Lines 424-428: TWO bot messages added BEFORE sendMessage is called: notificationMsg (line 424) and userConfirmMsg (line 427)

LlmChatModelHelper.sendMessage() called at line 432 with notification string, AFTER notificationMsg and userConfirmMsg have already been added to messages list. onThinking callback (line 488) and onToken callback (line 434) both append to messages.indexOfLast{!it.isUser} — which is userConfirmMsg

onThinking callback: mainHandler.post { val lastBot = messages.indexOfLast { !it.isUser }; if(lastBot>=0) msg.copy(thinkingText = msg.thinkingText + thinking) }. This appends thinking to userConfirmMsg which already contains workspaceInfo text

onToken callback: mainHandler.post { val lastBot = messages.indexOfLast { !it.isUser }; messages.mapIndexed{... msg.copy(text = msg.text + token) } }. Appends response tokens to userConfirmMsg (same message already containing workspace confirmation text)

LazyColumn items(messages) renders each message separately. AnimatedVisibility at line 928 checks message.thinkingText.isNotEmpty() to show ThinkingBubble. Since notificationMsg has empty thinkingText and userConfirmMsg has the thinking content, the thinking bubble shows on wrong message or thinking appears stuck

sendMessageAgentic() with isAgenticMode=true. When tool calls occur (line 409-431), the loop does doNextTurn('Based on previous tool result...'). The accumulatedText (line 438-439) combines thought and text channels. Multiple turns can cause repeated tool calls making messages appear stuck

ChatMessage data class: thinkingText and isThinkingDone are separate fields. isThinkingDone only set true in onDone callback (line 456). If thinking never completes properly or tool loop continues, isThinkingDone stays false causing 'stuck' appearance

assumptions: The 'two locked/stuck messages' are: (1) userConfirmMsg showing workspaceInfo text with response tokens appended, and (2) the same or another message showing thinking bubble with accumulated thinking that never completes

The notificationMsg at line 424 receives the thinking bubble display logic ( AnimatedVisibility checks thinkingText.isNotEmpty() ) but its thinkingText stays empty because thinking is appended to userConfirmMsg
When the model calls readWorkspaceFile tool to read the PDF markdown, this may trigger multiple agent turns, each adding to accumulatedText on userConfirmMsg
acceptance_criteria: When a PDF is sent, exactly ONE bot message appears before thinking starts
The thinking bubble appears on the same message that receives the final response text
No message shows workspace confirmation text AND response tokens mixed together
No message shows accumulated thinking text from multiple agent turns on the same message before the response completes
risks: Changing the message flow may require restructuring how PDF results are communicated to the model vs displayed to the user
The notification-based approach requires the model to call readWorkspaceFile tool — if the model doesn't call the tool or calls it multiple times, the message flow could still be wrong
The agentic loop in sendMessageAgentic can cause multiple turns which compound the message accumulation issue
test_plan: Send a PDF and observe the message list — verify only ONE bot message appears initially (not two)
Verify the thinking bubble appears on the same message that eventually shows the full response
Verify no message contains both workspace info text AND response tokens mixed together
Check logcat for [AGENT] Turn messages to see if multiple turns are occurring during PDF flow

behavioral_verification.required_observation: Only ONE bot message should appear while the model processes the PDF. The thinking bubble should appear on that same message. The workspace confirmation should either be a separate user message or NOT mixed with response tokens.
evidence_required: Log statements showing messages list at PDF picker result (line 379, 406, 424, 427, 432)
THINKING-UI log entries showing which message idx receives thinking text
PDF-RESP-TOKEN entries showing which message idx receives response tokens
